summary: The non-auth OTL request family around 0x140629580..0x14062B329 is now mapped as launcher/session telemetry traffic sharing the same 0x268-byte request object, constructor, destructor, scheduler enqueue path, and request submitter used by desktop-auth.
confidence: medium-high for function roles, literals, object lifetime, and request fields; medium for exact caller-facing semantic names of the surrounding session object fields.

key_actions: Initializes string-like small-string sentinel bytes at +0x1F, +0x37, +0x4F, +0x67, +0xCF, +0xE7, and +0x267 to 0x17.
Zeros header/list vectors at +0x68, +0x70, +0x78 and response/body vectors at +0x80, +0x88, +0x90, +0x98, +0xA0, and +0xA8.
Seeds the +0x108 pointer with request +0x110, indicating an inline backing object or embedded allocator/list sentinel.
Initializes progress/callback fields +0x210, +0x218, +0x220, +0x230, +0x234, +0x238, +0x240, +0x248, and +0x250/+0x267 before returning.
Sets +0x224 to 0xA, +0x228 to 0x100, and +0x22C to -1, which gives the request callback default outgoing/result values before response parsing overwrites them.
request_constructor.lifetime_relationship: Most request producers allocate 0x268 bytes through 0x140793B40, call this initializer, later destroy through 0x14062B8C0, and free through 0x1407B8478.

empty_body_submitter.relationship_to_0x14062BCE0: This helper is a sibling of the already-mapped 0x14062BCE0 submitter. It writes request state 3, copies the endpoint into request +0x8, copies an empty string from 0x140B5DCB8 into request +0x250, enqueues through 0x14062CBE0, and swaps the returned async handle into request +0x240.

evidence: Calls 0x14062A760 with RCX loaded from global 0x140C4C9C8, a stack-composed endpoint/query string in RDX, and caller-provided string material in R8.
Allocates a 0x268 request, initializes it with 0x14062B7B0, installs it around object +0x98, and submits through the empty-body helper 0x14062BC20.
Builds a session/open path, conditionally appends a userHash query, allocates and initializes a 0x268 request, and submits through the full payload submitter with the literal JSON body {}.
Reuses the shared request destructor on unwind/replacement branches before freeing 0x268-byte request storage.
Formats close endpoints with session identity and duration material, optionally includes userHash, allocates and initializes a 0x268 request, and submits with the literal JSON body {}.
Uses the same 0x14062B8C0 plus 0x1407B8478 cleanup pair as auth and session/open builders.
Chooses among click, dismissal, and impression event path literals, composes a four-part endpoint with userHash, allocates and initializes a 0x268 request, and submits with the literal JSON body {}.
Direct caller 0x14062B014 passes an event/context pointer in RCX and a selected event entry in RDX.

string_targets.0x140B5DCB8: empty C-string used by 0x14062BC20 as the default request body

object_lifetime_update.request_size_evidence: win64c-otl-request-size-0268-immediates.json recovered 91 decoded uses of the 0x268 allocation/free size, including the auth builders and the session/event family mapped here.

opennxt_947_server_implications: The OpenNXT compatibility surface is wider than desktop-auth: the same OTL transport can issue session/open, session/close, and click/dismissal/impression telemetry requests.
These non-auth requests still use the same async request object and scheduler handle semantics, so server shims and packet/log correlation should not treat auth traffic as a unique transport.
Several session/event builders submit {} as a JSON-ish body while carrying most parameters in the endpoint path or query string.

The default constructor values now explain why parser/callback fields have safe nonzero defaults before a response arrives: +0x224 starts at 10, +0x228 at 0x100, +0x22C at -1, and +0x234/+0x240/+0x248 start clear.
next_targets: Map global 0x140C4C9C8 and adjacent session globals to name the session service root and upstream config producer.
Split the surrounding owner object fields around +0x58, +0x68, +0x90, +0x98, +0xA0, and +0xA8 so session request ownership is tied to concrete UI or launcher subsystems.
Follow direct empty-body submitter callers 0x14014E7E0, 0x14014F38C, 0x140287850, 0x14029C950, 0x14029D320, and 0x14059D1E6 to identify the remaining non-auth OTL request families.
